meta.title: vert

source.publication: Section 4: Roots, Prefixes, Suffixes and Foreign Words (owner-supplied reference)
source.usage_note: Personal study only. Every word and meaning transcribed faithfully; the teaching layer (mentor_note, discovery) is authored for CAT OS.

meta.prompt_version: in-repo authored (Word DNA milestone); WORD_DNA_BIBLE frameworks applied
meta.reviewer_notes: The smallest family in the batch: three words only, exactly as the source gives them. Two taught, one (ambivert) held out for transfer, combining vert with the already-introduced idea of ambi- meaning both sides. Owner review pending.

unit.label: vert
unit.mentor_note: Three words, one small choreography of turning one way, the other way, or both.

members.meaning: to turn your thoughts in both directions

discovery.understand_note: Vert means to turn. An introvert turns their attention inward; an extrovert turns it outward. The root describes a direction, not a personality on its own.

applies.prompt: Knowing vert means to turn, and ambi- means both sides, what do you think an ambivert is?

options.text: Someone whose attention turns both inward and outward, depending on the moment
Someone who never turns their attention anywhere
Someone who turns away from other people entirely
